title: Bo Burnham’s ‘Inside,’ Five New Movies to Stream, and the First Couple of ‘The Big Picture’
format: Sean and Amanda argue over whether Bo Burnham's Netflix special 'Inside' is a movie, hand out five streaming picks, work through a mailbag, and close with two listeners who met over Letterboxd.

segments.heading: Bo Burnham's 'Inside': Is It a Movie?

films.title: Inside
films.note: Burnham wrote, shot, edited, and directed it alone in one room over the pandemic. Amanda, a stated skeptic of sad-boy musical comedy, was won over; both land on movie status for its self-contained intentionality.

films.title: Eighth Grade
films.note: Burnham's feature debut. Amanda wasn't a fan; Sean marks it as where his interest in Burnham started.

films.title: On the Count of Three
films.note: Jerrod Carmichael's Sundance directorial debut, cited as a comedian-goes-serious swing neither host thought quite worked; Carmichael is a close friend of Burnham's.

films.title: Stop Making Sense
films.note: Named as one of the concert-film DNA strands Sean hears in the special's staging.

films.title: Anchorman
films.note: Shorthand for the old comedian career path (leave the stage, go make the broad mainstream comedy) that Sean thinks 'Inside' upends.

picks.title: Riders of Justice
picks.note: Anders Thomas Jensen's revenge drama-comedy with Mads Mikkelsen, a very different turn from Another Round. Both hosts liked its odd tonal collision of grief and buffoonery.

picks.title: Plan B
picks.note: Natalie Morales's raunchy teen road comedy on Hulu, chasing the morning-after pill. Winning leads in Kuhoo Verma and Victoria Moroles; Amanda flagged the politics as a little uneasy.

picks.title: Skull: The Mask
picks.note: A gross, well-executed Brazilian slasher Sean holds up against the elevated-horror wave. He promised Amanda she'll never watch it.

picks.title: Happily
picks.note: Ben David Grabinski's disorienting couples-in-an-Airbnb fantasy with Joel McHale and Kerry Bishé, plus a deep who's-that supporting cast. Its closing song sent Sean to the fifth pick.

picks.title: Streets of Fire
picks.note: Walter Hill's rock-and-roll fable, newly on Netflix, surfaced via a song in 'Happily.' Sean hadn't seen it in 20 years and reports it still kicks ass.

films.title: Visions of Eight
films.note: The 1972 Munich Olympics documentary by eight directors. Sean, Amanda, Chris Ryan, and producer Bobby Wagner recorded a commentary for Criterion's new Blu-ray, out June 22.

segments.heading: The Mailbag
segments.note: Oscar strategy, the Apatow guys, TV vs. film, box office, and Jaws

films.title: Dances with Wolves
films.note: Floated (as a hypothetical sequel from Taylor Sheridan) in the win-an-Oscar-at-any-cost question; Amanda swears it's more than you remember.

films.title: Field of Dreams
films.note: The Kevin Costner box Amanda wants to keep him in.

films.title: Foxcatcher
films.note: Sean's example of Steve Carell reaching for prestige; he rates Bennett Miller's film higher than most.

films.title: Steve Jobs
films.note: Seth Rogen as Wozniak, offered as evidence the Apatow guys keep circling seriousness.

films.title: Don't Look Up
films.note: Jonah Hill's next, an Adam McKay ensemble; hard to gauge how large the role is.

films.title: The 40-Year-Old Virgin
films.note: Raised in the who-owns-Steve-Carell question of Apatow vs. McKay vs. the Office universe.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: One of the few Netflix films that made it to a Criterion physical release, in Sean's link-rot argument for owning what you love.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Cited alongside Marriage Story as a purchasable Netflix title, the exception to the rule.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Another Netflix-to-Criterion title, and a punchline about historically significant movies that don't win.

films.title: Blow Out
films.note: Sean's example of memorializing fandom with a hard copy; a De Palma favorite he'd pull off the shelf.

films.title: Chinatown
films.note: Surfaces via Sam Wasson's The Big Goodbye in the best New Hollywood book question.

films.title: Black Widow
films.note: Box office prediction; the Disney+ Premier Access wrinkle makes it the odd Marvel proposition.

films.title: F9
films.note: On the table as a theaters-only tentpole.

films.title: Dune
films.note: Going to HBO Max but still feels like a big-ticket item.

films.title: No Time to Die
films.note: Theaters-only Bond, a safe bet among the year's biggest.

films.title: Top Gun: Maverick
films.note: Named 'Top Gun 2' in the 2021 box office guesses.

films.title: A Quiet Place Part II
films.note: Headed to Paramount+ soon; interesting as one of the first movies back.

films.title: Venom
films.note: The surprise first hit, cited to argue its sequel will be huge too.

films.title: Venom: Let There Be Carnage
films.note: Part of the busy October Sean expects.

films.title: Jaws
films.note: Would you CGI the shark to look more believable? Absolute no from both. Sean rewatched it the night before and loves the shark on the boat most.

segments.heading: The First Couple of 'The Big Picture'
segments.note: Ben and Erica, who met over Letterboxd

films.title: Mank
films.note: The shared enthusiasm that first connected them; Ben calls his relationship 'equal parts Mank and equal parts Sean Fennessey.'

films.title: Sicario
films.note: Ben watched one of Erica's favorites on purpose as an early signal; she only learned it was deliberate later.

films.title: Brief Encounter
films.note: His Valentine's Day review of it, which Erica read as a signal without realizing.

films.title: I Think I Love My Wife
films.note: Ben's Chris Rock review mentioned he was single; Erica took it as a hint to message him.

films.title: House
films.note: Nobuhiko Obayashi's 'Hausu,' their first movie watched together in person, five stars from both.

films.title: Under Siege
films.note: Their first long-distance Zoom-night date.

films.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 1
films.note: Erica's contested opinion: two stars for Vol. 1, which Ben can't abide.

films.title: Kill Bill: Vol. 2
films.note: Four stars from Erica, who's in it for the narrative and the heart, not the fight scenes.

films.title: Before Sunrise
films.note: The basis for Ben's viral joke Letterboxd-dating-app meme, with Hawke and Delpy's profiles.

films.title: The Lost City of Z
films.note: Amanda's movie-draft pick that she thought was a slam dunk: a James Gray movie beloved by her and the show, big in Europe, ignored at home.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: The crowd-pleaser that, Amanda jokes, wins the draft over her prestige picks anyway.

films.title: Ghost World
films.note: A core text for Ben and Erica; Ben says its disgust-but-yearning tone is the tone of their relationship.

films.title: Crumb
films.note: Named among Terry Zwigoff's range as Sean's example of an underserved filmmaker.

films.title: Bad Santa
films.note: The other end of Zwigoff's diverse, mainstream filmography.

films.title: The Love Witch
films.note: Anna Biller's film, raised by Erica as a fascinating director who gets little coverage; Sean once tried and failed to interview her for its release.

referenced.group: Named in the 'Riders of Justice' action-comedy riff
films: The Conjuring: The Devil Made Me Do It

referenced.group: Altman's New Hollywood, rattled off in the book question

TV: The Falcon and the Winter Soldier
Not a film: The Muppets Great Gatsby (a viral spec script)

Anchorman.title: Anchorman: The Legend of Ron Burgundy

I Think I Love My Wife.title: I Think I Love My Wife

Birds of Prey.title: Birds of Prey (and the Fantabulous Emancipation of One Harley Quinn)
